title: The most rational take on AI you’ll hear this year
keyIdeas:
**2-sentence summary:**  
Benedict Evans argues that AI is as transformative as the internet or mobile, but we are only in the “1997” phase—early, messy, and full of unknowns. He pushes back on doomsday job-loss predictions, emphasizing that historical automation cycles create new roles and that real-world adoption takes years, not weeks.

**Key takeaways:**  
- AI’s impact is comparable to the internet or mobile, not the industrial revolution; we are in an early, uncertain stage similar to 1997.  
- Job automation fears are overblown: every major technology has destroyed some jobs and created more, often in roles we couldn’t foresee.  
- Enterprise adoption is slow (18‑month sales cycles, long implementation); companies won’t fire everyone overnight.  
- Foundation model companies (OpenAI, Anthropic) likely lack pricing power because models are commoditized; the real value may accrue to the application layer (like cloud vs. Windows).  
- Distribution becomes the key moat: incumbents with existing user bases (Google, Meta, Apple) can spray adequate AI features everywhere, making it hard for startups to break through.  
- Anti‑AI sentiment mixes real concerns (e.g., deepfakes, job displacement) with misinformation (e.g., data‑center water use is only 0.017% of U.S. consumption).  
- The hardest part of a job is often not the task that can be automated (e.g., writing code) but the context, judgment, and human interaction (e.g., knowing what to build).  
- Practical advice: dive into AI, learn to use it, and understand what it can do—don’t retreat into moral superiority.

**Notable quotes, numbers, or claims worth remembering:**  
- “AI is as big a deal as the internet or mobile and only as big a deal as the internet or mobile.”  
- “We’re in 1997… most stuff doesn’t work yet… the people who have already taken whichever pill imagine everybody else is there.”  
- “You talk to these doomers on Twitter… they’re morons.”  
- “The model labs are selling a commodity… they don’t have network effects.”  
- Global mobile industry revenue ~$1 trillion/year, capex ~$200 billion – but stocks have gone nowhere for 25 years (analogy for model labs).  
- Data‑center water consumption: ~0.017% of total U.S. water use (Livermore Lab study, 2024).  
- Number of accountants has risen throughout the 20th century despite spreadsheets and ERP.  
- “What’s the task and what’s the job?” – automating a task ≠ automating the whole role.  
- “It depends” – his most common answer.
